query: Angular ControlValueAccessor vs custom two-way binding interview question

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: Angular ControlValueAccessor API
competitors.selectionReason: The ControlValueAccessor API is the official contract for custom Angular form controls, including touched and disabled propagation.
theirStrengths: It is the canonical definition of the CVA contract and the individual methods Angular Forms calls.
theirGaps: It is API-shaped and less explicit about why plain two-way binding fails in production forms.

realWorldUseCases:
Forms contract

ControlValueAccessor is not just another way to sync a value. It is the contract that makes a custom Angular component behave like a real form control inside Angular Forms. That is the production difference: validation, touched/dirty state, reset behavior, and disabled handling keep working instead of being rebuilt with fragile custom two-way bindings.
followUpConfusion:
Decision rule
  • Use simple input/output binding for isolated value sync outside Angular Forms.
  • Use CVA the moment the component must act like a real form control.
  • If disabled, touched, validation, or reset behavior matters, custom two-way binding stops being enough.

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.
followUpConfusion: Our updated page now answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly with a sharper rule, boundary, or failure pattern.

actionableExamples: Keep one compact worked example near the top so the answer stays faster to scan than a broader reference.

competitors.winReason: The updated page now wins on production framing and follow-up confusion while the competitor remains competitive on raw examples.

competitors.label: Angular Custom Form Controls - Complete Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This Angular University guide is the strongest tutorial competitor for turning a custom component into a real Angular form control via CVA.
theirStrengths: It is a direct long-form CVA tutorial with validation and forms integration examples.
theirGaps: It is broader and more implementation-heavy than the narrower interview prompt of CVA versus custom two-way binding.

competitors.selectionReason: This is a directly relevant community comparator because it exposes the exact production problem of custom value syncing drifting out of sync.
theirStrengths: It is focused on the real sync bug that happens when custom binding and CVA responsibilities blur together.
theirGaps: It is one bug thread rather than a compact framework-level comparison.
